type="application/ld+json">[{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"SheBrew 2026 | Support & Socialize 🍻","url":"https://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-wjhnswdb/events/313389738/","description":"Stay Connected
**Join our new Instagram for event highlights and early alerts: @comemakenewfriends**
**(https://www.instagram.com/comemakenewfriends)**

On March 7th, we’re heading to the SheBrew Festival to back the incredible female brewers and cider makers driving our local craft industry! **Kicking off Women’s History Month**, this is the perfect way to invest in our community while having a blast.

Get Your Tickets Here: ️
**(Session 2)** (https://act.hrc.org/page/184430/event/1?ea.tracking.id=sm_adv_OR_030726)
**Note: This link is for the official festival ticket ($40). This is the cost to enter the event, not a fee charged by our group.**

**The Cause** ️‍
100% of proceeds go to the Human Rights Campaign, supporting equality and the small businesses behind the craft. Beyond the cause, this is about getting offline to make new friends and build real, face-to-face connections. **We’re hitting the 2nd Session** for the peak energy of the day! ⚡

**The Details** ✨
• The Deal: $40 General Admission includes 12 beer tastings, a commemorative glass, and an annual HRC membership.

• **Important: Ensure you select the Second Session (4pm–7pm) when purchasing.**

• The Meetup: On the day of the festival, we’ll post a specific spot for the group to meet so we can walk through together.

**Photography and Video Notice**
We’ll be capturing the vibes for our community! By joining us, you consent to being in photos/videos. If you prefer to stay off camera, just let a host know when you arrive.","startDate":"2026-03-08T00:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Portland Art Museum","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"US","streetAddress":"1219 Southwest Park Avenue, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Come Make New Friends","url":"https://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-wjhnswdb/"},"performer":"Come Make New Friends"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Shoot Your Shot 🎱 (On & Off the Table 😉💃🕺)","url":"https://www.meetup.com/pdx-single-30-and-40-somethings/events/313608367/","description":"Ready to mix, mingle, and spark some chemistry? 💫👀

We’re kicking the night off at Ringlers 🎱🍻 where we’ll socialize over billiards, laugh, flirt, and break a few racks. Whether you’re a pool shark 🦈 or just there for the vibes 😌, it’s the perfect low pressure way to connect with other fun singles.

At 9PM sharp 🕘, we turn up the energy and head over to Fortune 🎶💃🕺 for DJ music, dancing, and electric late-night vibes ⚡🔥. This is your moment to really shoot your shot on the dance floor and beyond 😉

The Game Plan 🗓️:
🎱 Meet at Ringlers – Billiards & Mingling 🍻
🕘 9PM – Walk over together to Fortune 🚶‍♂️🚶‍♀️
🎧 DJ Beats + Dancing All Night 💃🕺
🍸 Drinks flowing
🔥 Chemistry building

Come solo 🙋‍♂️🙋‍♀️
Leave with a story 📖
Maybe even a number 📱😉

Dress sharp 👔👗
Bring confidence 😎
And remember you miss 100% of the shots you don’t take 🎯🔥","startDate":"2026-03-08T03:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-1-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"McMenamins Ringlers Pub","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"US","streetAddress":"1332 West Burnside Street, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"PDX Single 30 & 40 Somethings","url":"https://www.meetup.com/pdx-single-30-and-40-somethings/"},"performer":"PDX Single 30 & 40 Somethings"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"🎉Impromptu Afternoon Social at Market Street Pub🎉","url":"https://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-wjhnswdb/events/313638829/","description":"Join the community on Instagram: **@comemakenewfriends**

I believe that connecting with other people is what bring purpose and meaning to our lives. This event is a chance to get out of the house, socialize, and perhaps make a new friend or two!

There is a separate event posted at the Portland Art Museum starting at 4pm - so this event serves two purposes. First, to have an informal social and second to be a pre-event for the She Brew festival.

I realize this is posted only a few days before the event date. So, this is an impromptu gathering - and not entirely representative of most of the events in this group. But if you have no plans Saturday afternoon yet, and feel like being a little spontaneous, then come on out!

Since there will be no designated space for our group, I'll be wearing a Santa hat which should be easily recognizable.

I suggest thinking about some interests/hobbies/passions you may want to talk about for this event. I have found that a good start to finding friends is to share a hobby or interest or passion.

I hope to see you there!

**Photography and Video Notice**
Please note that photos and videos may be taken during the event for promotional and community purposes. By attending, you consent to being photographed and recorded. However, if you prefer not to appear in any media, please let the organizer know upon arrival, as we are happy to accommodate that request.

**Join Us On Instagram**
Stay connected between events by following us on Instagram. We share photos, updates, behind-the-scenes moments and highlights from our amazing community. Come say hi and tag yourself in our photos.

@comemakenewfriends","startDate":"2026-03-07T22:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"McMenamin's Market Street Pub","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"1526 SW 10th Ave, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Come Make New Friends","url":"https://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-wjhnswdb/"},"performer":"Come Make New Friends"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Polish Potluck Dinner (Option A)","url":"https://www.meetup.com/international-cooking-and-dining-club/events/313368117/","description":"This Polish potluck is packed with gloriously unpronounceable, exotic dishes - perfect for food adventurers who see “I’ve never eaten that before” as an invitation, not a warning.

But the real secret ingredient isn’t the food, it’s the people. In this group, culinary explorers crack open a cookbook, tackle a new recipe, then proudly share it with 40 strangers who are just waiting to become friends. That takes confidence and generosity - exactly the kind of people you want to sit next to, toast with, and build friendships with. This is why you joined MeetUp in the first place.

Rules of the Road to Kraków:

📝 RSVP & Commit
If you’re unsure, skip this round. This one’s for full‑send food fanatics who already taste the pierogi of their dreams.

🍲 Bring a Handmade Dish
That means no plastic containers with suspiciously perfect labels. We want recipes that survived panic, indecision, and the “I’ll just add more butter” phase.

🥤 Bring Your Own Beverage & Serving Utensils
Help us avoid the ancient potluck ritual of “who has a spoon?” By bringing your own, you save your dish from being served with mystery utensils from someone’s glove compartment.

🧾 Post Your Dish in the Comments Section
Help us avoid the Great Cabbage Roll Clone Army. If everyone brings the same thing, we’ll need name tags for the casseroles just to tell them apart.

📅 Need to cancel? Tell us three days ahead of time
Last‑minute no‑shows make our carefully planned dumpling math implode. Cancel early so we don’t light a candle for your empty chair.

⚠️ Known Side Effects:
\- 🧠 Developing a mysterious ability to pronounce words with seven consonants in a row\.
\- 🎶 Randomly humming Polish folk tunes while waiting in line at the grocery store\.
\- 🧈 Calmly accepting that “too much butter” is no longer a concept you understand\.

Today's “why not?” can become tomorrow’s favorite memory. Join us and come break bread with some amazing people.","startDate":"2026-03-08T01:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Annette & Graeme's Home","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"655 SE 68th Avenue, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"International Cooking and Dining Club","url":"https://www.meetup.com/international-cooking-and-dining-club/"},"performer":"International Cooking and Dining Club"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Open Play 2.5-3.0 in NW Portland Close to Downtown","url":"https://www.meetup.com/learn-pickleball-pdx/events/313515175/","description":"Open Play 2.5-3.0 in NW Portland Close to Downtown

**Our events have more than 500 5-star reviews on Meetup!**

Open play indoors in a convenient and gorgeous location near downtown Portland! No need to trek to the suburbs to find a good game. We have two nets in a large, roomy and well-lit gym with high ceilings and plenty of space. Play will be Paddle Rotation or Round Robin or similar method based on number of players. Limited to 12 players, waiver required to play.

Payment is due with your RSVP. We have a “first to pay, first to play” policy to avoid no-shows, and your spot is not confirmed until payment is received. It’s $15 per person via Venmo to:
@Robert-Wolfe-28
Please contact me to make other arrangements.

If you pay but cannot attend, you have one opportunity to attend the same session in the future. After two no-shows you will need to pay again to take any session. No refunds starting 24 hours before an event.

Minimum of three players to run the session (Coordinator will play as needed), The session is coordinated by **Bob Wolfe**, 4.0 player and Level 1 Certified Instructor through the International Pickleball Teaching Professionals. Waiver is required to play, 18+ only please.
Please contact Bob Wolfe with any questions! [[Pballpdx@gmail.com](http://Pballpdx@gmail.com/)]([mailto:Pballpdx@gmail.com](http://mailto:Pballpdx@gmail.com/)) or 541-228-1634","startDate":"2026-03-07T22:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-2-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Hillside Community Center","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"653 NW Culpepper Terrace, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Learn Pickleball PDX","url":"https://www.meetup.com/learn-pickleball-pdx/"},"performer":"Learn Pickleball PDX"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Lucky Lab Pub Hawthorne Portlandia Spanish Conversation group","url":"https://www.meetup.com/the-portlandia-spanish-school-conversation-group/events/313345245/","description":"Hola, every Saturday at 1:00 PM we meet at the Lucky Lab Pub on SE Hawthorne Para practicar español y conocer nuevos amigos.
To make a reservation (RSVP) is necessary to participate in our group, the limit is 20 people !","startDate":"2026-03-07T21:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-2-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Lucky Labrador Brew Pub","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"915 SE Hawthorne Blvd, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"The Portlandia Spanish School Conversation Group","url":"https://www.meetup.com/the-portlandia-spanish-school-conversation-group/"},"performer":"The Portlandia Spanish School Conversation Group"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Skills and Drills: Stop Stinking at Dinking!","url":"https://www.meetup.com/learn-pickleball-pdx/events/313420251/","description":"**Our Sessions Have 1,000+ 5-Star Reviews!**

In a skillful pickleball game, half of the shots or more are dinks! conversely, you will not become a skillful player without getting good at dinking. So why don't you practice dinking more?

We will use the ball machine to learn forehand and backhand dinks cross court and down the line, including using topspin and bottom spin. Then, we will drill those skills through games and dink competitions. You WILL emerge as a much better dinker than you are today, and it WILL help you win more games.

Cost is $30 per player. Payment is due in advance via Venmo to: @Robert-Wolfe-28

18+ only please, waiver required to participate. Instructor is a 4.0 player and a Level 1 Certified Pickleball Instructor with the IPTPA.

Please contact me with questions!
Bob Wolfe
541-228-1634
Pballpdx@gmail.com","startDate":"2026-03-08T00:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-2-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Hillside Community Center","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"653 NW Culpepper Terrace, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Learn Pickleball PDX","url":"https://www.meetup.com/learn-pickleball-pdx/"},"performer":"Learn Pickleball PDX"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Practice Mandarin Chinese at "Market of Choice" on SE Belmont","url":"https://www.meetup.com/portlandmandarin/events/313599770/","description":"Market of Choice on SE Belmont has a mezzanine with a great view. *(The mezzanine is shown at the top of the photo)*. You can reach the mezzanine by stairs or elevator. It's fairly quiet up there, thus condusive to practicing your Mandarin Chinese language skills while engaging in interesting conversations with this friendly group. Coffee and food are available in the market.

Native and non-native speakers are welcome to attend. All skill levels from beginners to experts are cordially invited. Hope to see you there.

Free basement parking is available under the market","startDate":"2026-03-07T21:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"1090 SE Belmont St","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"1090 SE Belmont St, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Portland Mandarin Group","url":"https://www.meetup.com/portlandmandarin/"},"performer":"Portland Mandarin Group"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"Dinner at Taste of Sichuan - Beaverton","url":"https://www.meetup.com/cantonesesocialpdx/events/313291364/","description":"**Join us** for our next dinner gathering at **Taste of Sichuan** in Beaverton. Their address is 16261 NW Cornell Rd, Beaverton, OR 97006.

Our Dinner Meetups are a great way for you to make new friends and explore new places around the PDX Metro Area.

Located in the Tanasbourne area of Beaverton, Taste of Sichuan recently reopened after a long closure. This has been one of the more popular restaurants in the area, and we now have a chance to visit and support them once again!

*“At Taste of Sichuan, a sprawling Beaverton restaurant dedicated to the throbbing spices of Southwest China’s Sichuan region, the veil is lifted, with a not-so-secret “Wild Side” menu offering some adventurous flavors and authentic bites for American audiences.”*
**—Portland Monthly Eat Beat**

Come support our local Chinese community. Our reservation is for up to 20 people.

FYI - For our dinner meet ups, each person picks a dish that everyone shares, and then we split the check evenly between everyone who attends.

Please be sure to keep your RSVP updated if your plans change.","startDate":"2026-03-08T02:30:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-5-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Taste of Sichuan","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Beaverton","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"US","streetAddress":"16261 Nw Cornell Rd, Beaverton, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Cantonese Social Group of Portland","url":"https://www.meetup.com/cantonesesocialpdx/"},"performer":"Cantonese Social Group of Portland"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"First Saturday Contra Dance","url":"https://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-ccddsysh/events/313546955/","description":"This is a no-official-host contra dance at the Fulton Community Center, 68 SW Miles Street in Portland. All are welcome!

Tonight's band is **Pleasantly Frantic**, a 5-member ensemble known for their dramatic style; calling by **Noah Grunzweig.** This dance is being sponsored as a birthday celebration so it's free with an optional donation to IRCO. Lesson for newcomers at 7 pm, then dancing from 7:30 to 10:30 with a short break halfway through.

Bring water, clothing appropriate for indoor aerobic activity and smooth-soled shoes for dancing. Please do not wear scented products.

Cost: $15 is the usual suggested entry fee so that we can break even but tonight's dance is free!

Covid protocols: full vaccination and mask wearing is recommended but not required.

**Note:** There are always plenty of dancers on the floor irrespective of how many people RSVP via Meetup. For more information, go to: [www.portlandcountrydance.org](www.portlandcountrydance.org).","startDate":"2026-03-08T03:00:00.000Z","endDate":"","eventStatus":"https://schema.org/EventScheduled","image":"/images/fallbacks/redesign/group-cover-2-square.webp","eventAttendanceMode":"https://schema.org/OfflineEventAttendanceMode","location":{"@type":"Place","name":"Fulton Park Community Center","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Portland","addressRegion":"OR","addressCountry":"us","streetAddress":"68 SW Miles St, Portland, OR"}},"organizer":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Contra Dancing","url":"https://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-ccddsysh/"},"performer":"Contra Dancing"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Event","name":"D&D 5e, The Temple of Elemental Evil","url":"https://www.meetup.com/rpgpdx/events/313576718/","description":"The Temple of Elemental Evil

The classic award winning campaign has been reincarnated by Goodman Games. The authors interviewed the TSR people involved with the development of the classic ToEE. There's more side quests and lore from before the classic campaign was released as the module T 1-4.

Is everyone you meet who they say? There's political maneuvering, spies, plots, and something once sealed away stirring in the darkness.

Bandit raids and sightings of evil humanoids in Veluna are on the rise. There's fortunes to be made and renown for new adventures to be lauded as heroes. Are you brave enough to set out on a path of perils and earn your place in the ballads of heroes?

Join the ranks of novice adventures, gather your gear, and head to the capital of Veluna.

No cost to play. You can support the store by simply making a snack purchase. #onetrust-banner-sdk,
#onetrust-consent-sdk {
  border-radius: 24px !important;
  overflow: hidden;
  box-shadow: 0px 4px 12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.07) !important; /* X=0, Y=4, Blur=12, 7% opacity */
  border: 1px solid #EBEBEE !important;
  background-color: #FFFFFF !important; /* banner background color */
  background-clip: padding-box;
  color: #69696C !important; /* main text color */
  z-index: 99999 !important; /* ensures it's always on top */
}

/* === TEXT & LINKS === */
#onetrust-banner-sdk p,
#onetrust-banner-sdk span,
#onetrust-banner-sdk div,
#onetrust-consent-sdk p,
#onetrust-consent-sdk span,
#onetrust-consent-sdk div {
  color: #69696C !important; /* main text color */
}

#onetrust-banner-sdk a,
#onetrust-consent-sdk a {
  color: #7661D2 !important; /* link text color */
}

/* === BUTTONS === */
#onetrust-accept-btn-handler,
#onetrust-reject-all-handler,
.ot-sdk-container .ot-sdk-button {
  background-color: #353538 !important; /* Accept & Reject button background */
  color: #FFFFFF !important; /* button text color */
  border-radius: 24px !important;
  border: none !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-btn-handler {
  background-color: #FFFFFF !important; /* Manage Preferences button background */
  color: #353538 !important; /* Manage Preferences text color */
  border: 1px solid #353538 !important; /* Manage Preferences border color */
  border-radius: 24px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-btn-handler:hover {
  background-color: #F7F7F8 !important; /* hover effect for settings button */
}

/* === ACCORDION STYLING === */
.ot-accordion-layout,
.ot-accordion,
.ot-accordion-content {
  background-color: #FFFFFF !important; /* Accordion background color */
}

/* === FOCUS STATES === */
#onetrust-banner-sdk button:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk button:focus,
#onetrust-banner-sdk a:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk a:focus {
  outline: 2px solid #353538 !important; /* focus border color */
  outline-offset: 2px;
  border-radius: 4px;
}

/* === MOBILE-SPECIFIC STYLING === */
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  #onetrust-banner-sdk,
  #onetrust-consent-sdk {
    right: auto !important;
    width: 90% !important; /* margin on sides */
    overflow-y: auto !important; /* scroll if text is too long */
    z-index: 99999 !important; /* stays above other sticky elements */
  }
}

2026 | Support & Socialize 🍻","dateTime":"2026-03-07T16:00:00-08:00","description":"Stay Connected
**Join our new Instagram for event highlights and early alerts: @comemakenewfriends**
**(https://www.instagram.com/comemakenewfriends)**

On March 7th, we’re heading to the SheBrew Festival to back the incredible female brewers and cider makers driving our local craft industry! **Kicking off Women’s History Month**, this is the perfect way to invest in our community while having a blast.

Get Your Tickets Here: ️
**(Session 2)** (https://act.hrc.org/page/184430/event/1?ea.tracking.id=sm_adv_OR_030726)
**Note: This link is for the official festival ticket ($40). This is the cost to enter the event, not a fee charged by our group.**

**The Cause** ️‍
100% of proceeds go to the Human Rights Campaign, supporting equality and the small businesses behind the craft. Beyond the cause, this is about getting offline to make new friends and build real, face-to-face connections. **We’re hitting the 2nd Session** for the peak energy of the day! ⚡

**The Details** ✨
• The Deal: $40 General Admission includes 12 beer tastings, a commemorative glass, and an annual HRC membership.

• **Important: Ensure you select the Second Session (4pm–7pm) when purchasing.**

• The Meetup: On the day of the festival, we’ll post a specific spot for the group to meet so we can walk through together.

**Photography and Video Notice**
We’ll be capturing the vibes for our community! By joining us, you consent to being in photos/videos. S.","memberPhoto":{"__ref":"PhotoInfo:323105212"}},"Event:313638829":{"__typename":"Event","id":"313638829","title":"🎉Impromptu Afternoon Social at Market Street Pub🎉","dateTime":"2026-03-07T14:00:00-08:00","description":"Join the community on Instagram: **@comemakenewfriends**

I believe that connecting with other people is what bring purpose and meaning to our lives. This event is a chance to get out of the house, socialize, and perhaps make a new friend or two!

There is a separate event posted at the Portland Art Museum starting at 4pm - so this event serves two purposes. First, to have an informal social and second to be a pre-event for the She Brew festival.

I realize this is posted only a few days before the event date. So, this is an impromptu gathering - and not entirely representative of most of the events in this group. But if you have no plans Saturday afternoon yet, and feel like being a little spontaneous, then come on out!

Since there will be no designated space for our group, I'll be wearing a Santa hat which should be easily recognizable.

I suggest thinking about some interests/hobbies/passions you may want to talk about for this event. I have found that a good start to finding friends is to share a hobby or interest or passion.

I hope to see you there!

**Photography and Video Notice**
Please note that photos and videos may be taken during the event for promotional and community purposes. By attending, you consent to being photographed and recorded. However, if you prefer not to appear in any media, please let the organizer know upon arrival, as we are happy to accommodate that request.

**Join Us On Instagram**
Stay connected between events by following us on Instagram. We share photos, updates, behind-the-scenes moments and highlights from our amazing community. Come say hi and tag yourself in our photos.

A)","dateTime":"2026-03-07T17:00:00-08:00","description":"This Polish potluck is packed with gloriously unpronounceable, exotic dishes - perfect for food adventurers who see “I’ve never eaten that before” as an invitation, not a warning.

But the real secret ingredient isn’t the food, it’s the people. In this group, culinary explorers crack open a cookbook, tackle a new recipe, then proudly share it with 40 strangers who are just waiting to become friends. That takes confidence and generosity - exactly the kind of people you want to sit next to, toast with, and build friendships with. This is why you joined MeetUp in the first place.

Rules of the Road to Kraków:

📝 RSVP & Commit
If you’re unsure, skip this round. This one’s for full‑send food fanatics who already taste the pierogi of their dreams.

🍲 Bring a Handmade Dish
That means no plastic containers with suspiciously perfect labels. We want recipes that survived panic, indecision, and the “I’ll just add more butter” phase.

🥤 Bring Your Own Beverage & Serving Utensils
Help us avoid the ancient potluck ritual of “who has a spoon?” By bringing your own, you save your dish from being served with mystery utensils from someone’s glove compartment.

🧾 Post Your Dish in the Comments Section
Help us avoid the Great Cabbage Roll Clone Army. If everyone brings the same thing, we’ll need name tags for the casseroles just to tell them apart.

📅 Need to cancel? Tell us three days ahead of time
Last‑minute no‑shows make our carefully planned dumpling math implode. Cancel early so we don’t light a candle for your empty chair.

⚠️ Known Side Effects:
\- 🧠 Developing a mysterious ability to pronounce words with seven consonants in a row\.
\- 🎶 Randomly humming Polish folk tunes while waiting in line at the grocery store\.
\- 🧈 Calmly accepting that “too much butter” is no longer a concept you understand\.

Today's “why not?” can become tomorrow’s favorite memory. Downtown","dateTime":"2026-03-07T14:00:00-08:00","description":"Open Play 2.5-3.0 in NW Portland Close to Downtown

**Our events have more than 500 5-star reviews on Meetup!**

Open play indoors in a convenient and gorgeous location near downtown Portland! No need to trek to the suburbs to find a good game. We have two nets in a large, roomy and well-lit gym with high ceilings and plenty of space. Play will be Paddle Rotation or Round Robin or similar method based on number of players. Limited to 12 players, waiver required to play.

Payment is due with your RSVP. We have a “first to pay, first to play” policy to avoid no-shows, and your spot is not confirmed until payment is received. It’s $15 per person via Venmo to:
@Robert-Wolfe-28
Please contact me to make other arrangements.

If you pay but cannot attend, you have one opportunity to attend the same session in the future. After two no-shows you will need to pay again to take any session. No refunds starting 24 hours before an event.

Minimum of three players to run the session (Coordinator will play as needed), The session is coordinated by **Bob Wolfe**, 4.0 player and Level 1 Certified Instructor through the International Pickleball Teaching Professionals. Waiver is required to play, 18+ only please.
Please contact Bob Wolfe with any questions! and Drills: Stop Stinking at Dinking!","dateTime":"2026-03-07T16:00:00-08:00","description":"**Our Sessions Have 1,000+ 5-Star Reviews!**

In a skillful pickleball game, half of the shots or more are dinks! conversely, you will not become a skillful player without getting good at dinking. So why don't you practice dinking more?

We will use the ball machine to learn forehand and backhand dinks cross court and down the line, including using topspin and bottom spin. Then, we will drill those skills through games and dink competitions. You WILL emerge as a much better dinker than you are today, and it WILL help you win more games.

Cost is $30 per player. Payment is due in advance via Venmo to: @Robert-Wolfe-28

18+ only please, waiver required to participate. Instructor is a 4.0 player and a Level 1 Certified Pickleball Instructor with the IPTPA.

Please contact me with questions!
Bob Wolfe
541-228-1634
